headline: Markets Mixed as Warsh Era Begins, Iran Deal Progresses, and AI Chip Dynamics Shift
summary: US indices saw mixed performance with NASDAQ down 1.2% as FOMC caution weighed on tech, while DOW gained 0.6%. Geopolitical tensions eased with progress on a US-Iran peace deal, impacting oil prices. Key developments in AI chips included Google's potential partnership with Samsung and new US export controls on advanced AI models.

bullets.lead: US-Iran peace deal progress eased Middle East tensions and impacted oil markets.

segments.text: The US government is reportedly set to waive sanctions on Iranian oil following a peace agreement, with President Trump indicating a signing ceremony could occur in Europe this weekend.

sources.title: Sizing Up Trump’s Iran Deal
Trump hails Iran deal but conflict continues to cast long shadow over global economy
Trump Says U.S. May Impose Tolls In Strait Of Hormuz If Peace Deal Fails
Rahm Emanuel says Trump ‘got schooled’ by Iran in bad ceasefire deal

segments.text: This de-escalation led to a drop in WTI crude to $81 and a broad market rally on expectations of Hormuz Strait reopening.

sources.title: Oil Glut Bets Are Back in Play as Crude Sinks After US-Iran Deal
Iran's Tasnim news agency says Hormuz will not reopen until Lebanon ceasefire holds, oil waivers issued
Mines, Logistics and Deep Uncertainty Threaten a Middle East Oil Rebound
Deal or no deal, oil prices will stay volatile for months

bullets.lead: The US government has initiated export controls on Anthropic's advanced AI models.

segments.text: Anthropic's top-tier AI models, Mythos 5 and Fable 5, have been blocked from access by foreign governments, companies, and individuals due to national security concerns, marking the first such intervention by the US government.

sources.title: Exclusive: Trump tells "The Axios Show" that Anthropic was a national security threat

segments.text: This action follows claims of jailbreaking the Mythos model's safeguards, with Amazon CEO Andy Jassy reportedly expressing concerns over the implications.

bullets.lead: China is aggressively pushing for wider adoption of New Energy Vehicle (NEV) heavy trucks.

segments.text: The Chinese Ministry of Transport aims to increase NEV heavy truck penetration to 40% by 2030, up from 29% in 2025, targeting over 1.6 million units in operation.

Plans include electrifying over 80% of fixed short-distance transport routes and building 3,000 charging/battery swap stations and 30,000 km of green freight networks, supported by financial incentives and new business models like battery leasing.

bullets.lead: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh debuts amid rate cut speculation.

segments.text: The new Fed Chair will oversee the FOMC meeting on June 18th (3 AM KST), with market participants anticipating his stance on inflation and potential rate cuts, particularly given President Trump's desire for lower rates.

sources.title: 'Regime change but in a velvet glove': How Kevin Warsh has set out to remake the Fed
Warsh to review how Fed works after holding US interest rates at first meeting

segments.text: Warsh is expected to emphasize the Trimmed Mean PCE, which excludes volatile price changes, as a key inflation metric, though the broader impact of oil prices may complicate immediate rate cut decisions.

sources.title: Inflation Will Linger Despite U.S.-Iran Deal, British Officials Warn
Fed Chair Warsh expected to withhold 'dot' from central bank's interest rate outlook
Inflation unexpectedly steady as food price rises slow

bullets.lead: The Bank of Japan is expected to raise its benchmark interest rate.

segments.text: The BOJ is projected to increase rates from 0.75% to 1.0% today, marking the first time Japan's rates have reached 1% since 1995, with economists forecasting further hikes to 1.25% by year-end.

bullets.lead: May Producer Price Index (PPI) data showed mixed signals for inflation.

segments.text: While headline PPI surpassed forecasts due to energy prices, the core measure, excluding food and energy, came in lower than expected, alleviating concerns of widespread inflationary pressures.

sources.title: Inflation Will Linger Despite U.S.-Iran Deal, British Officials Warn
Inflation unexpectedly steady as food price rises slow

segments.text: Markets reacted positively to the contained core inflation and easing Middle East risks, leading to a decline in Treasury yields across all maturities.

bullets.lead: Google is exploring Samsung's 2nm process for its future AI chips.

segments.text: The Information reports Google is considering Samsung Electronics for partial production of its 10th-generation TPU 'Icefish,' specifically for the 2nm memory I/O die, while TSMC would handle the 1.4nm core.

sources.title: Why Google Star AI Researcher Joined OpenAI, New OpenClaw Competitor, Amazon’s AI Chip Advantage

segments.text: This potential diversification stems from TSMC's tight production capacity, driven by high demand for Nvidia's AI chips, offering Samsung a significant opportunity to strengthen its advanced foundry references.

sources.title: What Will Nvidia Stock's Price Be in 1 Year?

bullets.lead: Qualcomm is reportedly negotiating to acquire AI chip startup Tenstorrent.

segments.text: The potential acquisition of Jim Keller's RISC-V based Tenstorrent would allow Qualcomm to expand its AI chip capabilities and reduce reliance on Intel and ARM architectures.

bullets.lead: China's ambitious AI infrastructure self-reliance plan faces significant hurdles.

segments.text: Tom's Hardware highlights critical bottlenecks, including the 80% domestic sourcing requirement for AI chips, which strains SMIC's 7nm N+2 process already operating at over 93% utilization.

sources.title: US raises alarm over possible EUV machine in China, putting ASML on the defensive

segments.text: A shortage of HBM supply within China and limitations in domestic AI chip performance suggest the country may not meet its AI chip demand by 2030, potentially delaying its 2028 infrastructure completion target.

bullets.lead: The AI sector is driving a supercycle in memory and advanced interconnect technologies.

segments.text: The market is seeing strong demand for 1.6T optical transceivers, Co-Packaged Optics (CPO), and Electro-optic Light Source (ELSFP) technologies, essential for overcoming bandwidth bottlenecks in high-performance computing.
This demand, coupled with undersupply, is fueling significant year-over-year price increases for DRAM, Flash, and HBM, indicating a robust memory market supercycle.

bullets.lead: SpaceX successfully completed its IPO, attracting significant investor attention.

segments.text: The company debuted at $160.1 per share, achieving a market capitalization that places it as the 6th largest in the US, with expectations for inclusion in major indices like Russell 1000 and NASDAQ 100 by early July.

sources.title: ‘It’s Going To Be Glorious’—SpaceX And Tesla Suddenly Braced For A Game Changer

segments.text: Despite a shorter-than-standard lock-up period with staggered releases, limited float and anticipated ETF inflows are expected to support the stock, though most insider selling is concentrated in Q3.

bullets.lead: Nvidia successfully issued $25 billion in corporate bonds to fund AI investments.

segments.text: The bond offering saw overwhelming demand, with orders totaling $85 billion, underscoring continued investor confidence in Nvidia's AI-driven growth strategy and its ongoing investments in companies like Intel and Anthropic.

sources.title: What Will Nvidia Stock's Price Be in 1 Year?

bullets.lead: OpenAI is evaluating significant token price reductions to maintain competitiveness.

segments.text: To counter competition from Anthropic and address high computing costs, OpenAI is considering substantial price cuts for its enterprise customers, a move that could further pressure profitability for both companies.

The company is also pivoting from a chatbot-centric model to a "superapp" strategy, integrating coding tools, AI agents, and external applications to enhance its value proposition and drive higher-margin enterprise revenue.

sources.title: Amazon’s Movie Arm Abandons Film About OpenAI
Why Google Star AI Researcher Joined OpenAI, New OpenClaw Competitor, Amazon’s AI Chip Advantage
Luca Guadagnino’s Sam Altman movie dropped by Amazon after it announces OpenAI partnership

bullets.lead: Oracle's stock declined following an announcement of a substantial capital raise.

segments.text: The company plans to raise an additional $40 billion through bonds and $20 billion in new share issuance to fund its AI infrastructure and cloud investments, despite reporting strong earnings that confirmed an improvement cycle.

watchlist.name: Fed
watchlist.note: Watch for new Chair Kevin Warsh's communication style and stance on inflation, particularly his emphasis on Trimmed Mean PCE, at his inaugural FOMC meeting.

sources.title: 'Regime change but in a velvet glove': How Kevin Warsh has set out to remake the Fed
Warsh to review how Fed works after holding US interest rates at first meeting

watchlist.note: Monitor for post-IPO volatility and the impact of index inclusions and staggered lock-up expirations on its stock performance.

watchlist.note: Keep an eye on WTI crude as the US-Iran peace deal progresses and the potential reopening of the Strait of Hormuz could further influence global supply.

watchlist.note: Watch for further developments on its potential partnership with Google for next-gen TPU production, which could significantly boost its advanced foundry references.

watchlist.note: Monitor the impact of US government export controls on its advanced AI models and its competitive response to OpenAI's potential token price reductions.

sources.title: Exclusive: Trump tells "The Axios Show" that Anthropic was a national security threat

x_themes.summary: The market is seeing significant advancements and demand in optical transceivers, including 1.6T, Co-Packaged Optics (CPO), and Electro-optic Light Source (ELSFP) technologies, driven by AI data center needs. Companies like Sivers, OE Solutions, Marvell, and Jabil are key players in developing and supplying these next-gen optical architectures, with some concerns about potential oversupply being dismissed.

The memory market is experiencing a supercycle with significant year-over-year price increases for DRAM, Flash, and HBM, driven by strong AI demand and undersupply. Hyperscalers are projected to dramatically increase their capex allocation to memory, leading to potential upside surprises for companies like Micron and Kioxia, whose valuations are still considered low.

Advanced packaging, including glass substrates and Through-Glass Via (TGV) technology, is becoming critical for overcoming bandwidth bottlenecks and yield issues in high-performance AI chips by enabling tighter integration of smaller chiplets. Companies like LPKF and JNTC are developing key equipment and processes for glass-based advanced packaging, with major semiconductor clients and foundries like TSMC and Intel exploring these solutions.

The valuation of AI Large Language Model (LLM) companies, particularly Chinese frontier labs like Zhipu (Knowledge Atlas) and Minimax, is drawing attention, with some reaching high ARR figures. There are discussions around the competitive landscape with US players like Anthropic and OpenAI, and regulatory scrutiny on AI models and prediction markets.

The semiconductor industry is facing various supply chain bottlenecks and geopolitical pressures, including a shortage of E-glass fabric for PCBs (impacting Nvidia suppliers), a halt in tungsten material flow for PCB tools, and Taiwan's surging electricity demand driven by AI data centers and semiconductor manufacturing. US concerns about China's access to top chip tools also highlight geopolitical tensions in the supply chain.

The concept of AI compute becoming a commodity is emerging, with CME and ICE considering listing compute as a futures commodity, shifting focus to production bottlenecks in specialized tools and testing. Nvidia's Vera Rubin-based AI data centers are highlighted for their massive cost and power demands, driving the evolution of data center architecture and the need for efficient cooling solutions like microfluidics.

The space sector is seeing significant activity, including the anticipated float unlock timeline for SpaceX's IPO, and trading opportunities in companies like AST SpaceMobile ($ASTS) and Rocket Lab ($RKLB) based on batch deployment success and sector-specific influences.

GLP-1 receptor agonists and dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonists ($LLY, $NVO) are being explored for their potential to reduce impulsivity and addictive behaviors, including gambling and compulsive trading. While direct clinical evidence for gambling disorder is early, mechanistic neuroscience and alcohol-use-disorder trials suggest these drugs could make investors more measured by dampening cue-triggered urges and loss-chasing.
